records.ety: [OE. tripe, F. tripe; of uncertain origin; cf. Sp. & Pg. tripa, It. trippa, OD. tripe, W. tripa, Armor. stripen.]

defs: The large stomach of ruminating animals, when prepared for food.
quotes: How say you to a fat tripe finely broiled ?

defs: The entrails; hence, humorously or in contempt, the belly; -- generally used in the plural.
